vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct healthy specimens; the black pot contains only a single, struggling seedling/cutting. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)
inventory_reconciliation.New_Intervention: Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing in the black pot.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty. Soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant matter.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Black_Pot: Contains a single, etiolated, chlorotic sprout/cutting. It is not the registered species. High probability of failure.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Single Pothos leaf remains. The rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, potentially over-saturated. No fungal blooms visible, but soil texture suggests high organic content.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant presence of eggshell fragments in the black pot. A small electronic component (IC/chip) appears and disappears from the desk surface across the timeline.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: The sprout in the black pot shows slight postural change, leaning toward the light source.
temporal_deltas.T-2: Extreme low-light capture makes observation difficult, but the sprout appears to maintain its position.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: The sprout shows signs of dehydration or collapse; the leaf is drooping significantly compared to T-5.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. P1, P2, and P4 are effectively lost. The remaining Pothos leaf in P3 is the only viable biological material, though it is isolated. The 'sprout' in the black pot is likely a failed propagation attempt.
anomalies: Presence of electronic component (IC) on the desk surface.
Total absence of registered P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a failing indoor garden. The registry provided does not match the physical reality of the pots, which are largely empty or contain failed propagations. The Pothos leaf in the rabbit-anchored pot is the only healthy-looking element. The presence of eggshells suggests an amateur attempt at nutrient supplementation, which has not prevented the systemic loss of the primary specimens.
